title: Earthly Fermented Portuguese Apple Dumplings
description: A traditional Portuguese dessert featuring fermented apple juice and pillowy dumplings, wrapped in lotus leaves for a unique and nostalgic treat. This gluten-free-friendly dish is perfect for special occasions.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the apple juice, sugar, and yeast, stirring to dissolve the yeast.
Let the mixture sit at room temperature for 2 hours, or until it becomes frothy and bubbly.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the tapioca flour and eggs.
Add the melted butter to the flour mixture and stir until a smooth dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes, until it becomes elastic and pliable.
Divide the dough into 12 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Wrap each ball in a lotus leaf, securing with a toothpick if necessary.
Place the dumplings in a large pot and add the fermented apple juice mixture.
Simmer the dumplings over low heat for 20 minutes, or until they are cooked through and fluffy.
Serve the dumplings warm, garnished with additional sugar and apple juice if desired.
